recommendations.rationale: Only product with cost data (AUD 3,500 ex GST). Suggested price applies ~57% margin (AUD 1,995 gross margin), well above AUD 500 minimum threshold, aligned with typical networking switch markups in AU B2B channel. No current price visible in feed — requires confirmation before publishing.

pricing.pricingInsights: Insufficient data for confident pricing recommendations across the catalogue. Critical gaps: (1) No current pricing provided for any product — cannot evaluate change magnitude against the 15% max-change or AUD 5,000 approval thresholds. (2) Only 1 of 8 products has cost data (Mellanox NDR400 switch), preventing margin validation against the AUD 500 minimum on the remaining 7 SKUs. (3) Competitor pricing array is empty — no market benchmark for premium SKUs (H100/H200 SXM5, DGX H200, XE9680) which typically command AUD 40k–500k+ and are highly sensitive to competitive positioning. (4) Traffic signal is extremely weak: only 4 sessions in the trend window with zero product views recorded, so demand elasticity cannot be inferred. Recommended next steps: ingest current price list, attach landed cost (ex-GST) per SKU, and pull at least 2–3 AU/APAC competitor quotes for the H100/H200/DGX tier before any auto-apply pricing run. Until then, all pricing changes on flagship GPU and DGX SKUs should be routed to human approval regardless of confidence score.

opportunities.description: No competitor pricing data was provided in this analysis cycle, limiting visibility into market positioning for high-value SKUs such as NVIDIA H100/H200 SXM5, DGX H200, and Dell PowerEdge XE9680.
opportunities.action: Initiate competitor price scraping or manual benchmarking against key AU/APAC resellers (e.g., Leader Computers, Scorptec B2B, CDW, Insight, PNY partners) for the listed catalog before next analysis cycle.

opportunities.description: Mellanox InfiniBand NDR400 Switch is the only SKU with cost data (AUD 3,500 ex GST), providing a clear margin baseline for competitive pricing modelling.
opportunities.action: Establish a target sell price band (e.g., 3,950–4,500 AUD ex GST) to maintain the AUD 500 minimum margin while remaining competitive; flag for human review once competitor data is ingested.

opportunities.description: Extremely low session volume (4 sessions) indicates insufficient demand-side signal to validate competitive positioning.
opportunities.action: Defer aggressive repricing actions until traffic data normalises; focus on SEO/PPC and outbound B2B campaigns for flagship GPU and DGX lines.

competitor.summary: Insufficient competitor and traffic data to identify concrete pricing threats this cycle. Catalog is well-aligned with high-demand enterprise AI categories (Hopper-class GPUs, DGX, DPU, InfiniBand), but competitive intelligence inputs and session volume must be enriched before automated repricing can be confidently applied under the current 0.75 confidence threshold.
